title: Playground Command File
concept: A minimalist expert interface for Blueprint authors that treats the Playground as a single command file: a compact toolbar exposes structured menus, while authoring, runtime preview, Site Manager tools, saved management, gallery browsing, and portability flows unfold in one vertical stack.
flows: Start a new Playground from vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, .zip import, featured blueprints, or the full Blueprint gallery.
Author and run blueprint.json with copy link and bundle download controls before inspecting the runtime preview.
Save an unsaved Playground to browser storage or a local directory with visible file-copy progress.
Browse saved and unsaved Playgrounds, then rename or delete saved browser-backed sites.
Use Site Manager settings, files, Blueprint, database, logs, Homepage, WP Admin, export to GitHub, and .zip download from compact command surfaces.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
edit WordPress version, PHP version, and language settings
communicate destructive reset and stored Save & Reload behavior
file browser with create file, create folder, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
view, copy, download, and run the current Blueprint bundle
inspect database driver, path, and size
inspect Playground, WordPress, and PHP logs with empty states
